title: Clinical Alert Fatigue Analysis
spl:
index=clinical sourcetype="epic:alert"
| eval ack_sec=ack_time-_time
| stats median(ack_sec) as med_ack, count as fires, dc(alert_id) as rules by unit
| eval fires_per_bed=fires/bed_count
| sort -fires
description: Quantifies alert acknowledgement times and duplicate fires to guide CDS tuning and Joint Commission alarm safety.
value: Quantifies alert acknowledgement times and duplicate fires to guide CDS tuning and Joint Commission alarm safety.
implementation: Deprecate low-value rules; correlate with nurse staffing ratios from census feeds.
visualization: Bar chart (fires per bed), Box plot (ack times), Table (top noisy rules).
knownFalsePositives: Clinical alert fatigue from new order sets, EHR version upgrades, and QI projects that temporarily duplicate notifications; treat as workflow tuning.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: EHR alerting, middleware.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="epic:alert"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Deprecate low-value rules; correlate with nurse staffing ratios from census feeds.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=clinical sourcetype="epic:alert"
| eval ack_sec=ack_time-_time
| stats median(ack_sec) as med_ack, count as fires, dc(alert_id) as rules by unit
| eval fires_per_bed=fires/bed_count
| sort -fires
```

Understanding this SPL

**Clinical Alert Fatigue Analysis** — Quantifies alert acknowledgement times and duplicate fires to guide CDS tuning and Joint Commission alarm safety.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="epic:alert"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): EHR alerting, middleware. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: clinical; **sourcetype**: epic:alert. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=clinical, sourcetype="epic:alert". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ack_sec** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by unit** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **fires_per_bed** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (fires per bed), Box plot (ack times), Table (top noisy rules).
grandmaExplanation: In Healthcare, we pay close attention to "Clinical Alert Fatigue Analysis" in our day-to-day work so that fraud, waste, and compliance issues do not slip by quietly. We help teams focus on the few situations that need a person, not every routine blip in the data.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model. Optional mapping to CIM, if you adopt it, is a separate follow-on project.
